steps.title: Locate the sentence's new role
steps.content: Before the underlined sentence, the passage explains that trademarks can be owned and gives "Kleenex" as an example of a specific product name. The underlined sentence begins with "However" and introduces a new point: a trademark can be taken away if it stops being specific. The cellophane example then develops that point.

steps.title: Match the discussion shift
steps.content:
• Choice A - It concludes the text's argument about how the Trademark Act of 1946 can be improved.: The text explains the Act but does not argue for improving it.
  • Choice B - It completes the text's comparison of trademark laws in the US to laws in other countries.: No laws from other countries appear in the passage.
  • Choice C - It adds to the text's description of products made by Kimberly-Clark.: Kimberly-Clark is part of the earlier Kleenex example, but the underlined sentence shifts away from that example.
The sentence starts the discussion of trademarks being taken away. The answer is D.
